title: bunium cylindricum subsp. longipes
intro: Bunium cylindricum subsp. longipes, also known as the Long-stemmed Bunium, is a sub-species of the Bunium cylindricum plant, a type of umbelliferous plant in the carrot family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The Long-stemmed Bunium has been reported to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural diuretic to help reduce water retention. Herbal preparations of the Long-stemmed Bunium can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine in some regions to treat various health conditions.
